name: Mandarin orange (Clementine, Tangerine) (food medium)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant sugar; sucrose:glucose:fructose ~2:1:1 typical of citrus

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Sum of free sugars; carbohydrate 13.3 g, fiber 1.8 g

components.name: citric acid (L-citrate)
components.source: Cano-Lamadrid triploid-mandarin juice study (citric 8.53-13.18 g/L); Springer Tangerine chapter (Clementine titratable acidity 0.6-1.0% as citric)
components.evidence: PRIMARY organic acid in mandarin (85-90% of total organic acids); major co-carbon/energy source. Value = ~1% acidity as citric on whole-fruit basis

components.name: malic acid (L-malate)
components.evidence: Second organic acid; midpoint ~0.68 g/L juice ~= 0.07 g/100g

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: citrus juice literature (orange quinic 5.65-9.17 g/L; present in mandarin, lower)
components.evidence: Present in citrus juice but not quantified specifically for mandarin here; include as a real minor organic acid, amount unresolved

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: mandarin juice literature (minor organic acid after citric/malic)
components.evidence: Minor organic acid, present but not reliably quantified for mandarin

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; midpoint ~0.07 g/L juice

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Ascorbic acid; also an organic acid

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Pectin-rich; largely non-fermentable by non-degrading strains

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive toxic contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen; biologically implausible in citrus fruit; FooDB predicted/contaminant

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial fermentation food preservative additive, not a native mandarin metabolite

remove.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-physiological stereoisomer artifact; real isoleucine already listed

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element per curation policy

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: ultratrace non-nutritive element, not a defining growth nutrient; unquantified

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: ultratrace non-nutritive element, not a growth nutrient; unquantified

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: trace contaminant element, not a measured growth nutrient here

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt not measured for mandarin; FooDB-predicted trace, implausible at nutritive levels

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace/contaminant element, not quantified as a growth nutrient

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: ultratrace element, not measured for mandarin; FooDB-predicted

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, was absent as a quantified acid; L-malate was the only acid present)
quinic acid (citrus organic acid, present, listed unresolved)
individual sugar amounts (sucrose 6.1, glucose 2.1, fructose 2.4 g/100g)
full quantitative amino-acid, vitamin, mineral and fatty-acid panel from USDA
keep_uncovered: (R)-Oxypeucedanin (genuine citrus terpenes, flavanone glycosides and aroma volatiles; no BiGG metabolite, correctly uncovered)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169105 (SR Legacy: Tangerines, mandarin oranges, raw) - full sugar/amino-acid/vitamin/mineral/fatty-acid panel

Cano-Lamadrid et al. / triploid late-season mandarin juice study (Food Research International, S0963996912002840): mandarin juice organic acids citric 8.53-13.18 g/L, malic 447.76-902.48 mg/L, ascorbic 286.82-389.02 mg/L, oxalic 47.04-98.03 mg/L
Springer Tangerine (Citrus reticulata) chapter doi:10.1007/978-3-031-37534-7_6: Clementine/Satsuma titratable acidity 0.6-1.0% as citric; citric = 85-90% of total organic acids
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a growth medium are the free sugars (sucrose ~6.1, fructose ~2.4, glucose ~2.1 g/100g) plus citric acid (~0.9 g/100g, the mandarin's principal organic acid and the key metabolite missing from the FooDB profile, which carried only L-malate). Removed 11 FooDB noise/contaminant entries (arsenate, ethynylestradiol, epsilon-polylysine, Al/Si/B/Ni/Co/Cr/Mo trace elements, erythro-isoleucine artifact). Genuine citrus flavanone glycosides (hesperidin, narirutin) and terpene volatiles retained as uncovered.
